# Damilare Osibanjo — Full Stack Systems Developer & Web Programmer
Site URL: https://devdami.varityweb.com
LLMs.md: https://devdami.varityweb.com/llms.md

## Profile Summary
Damilare Osibanjo (born 2008) is a 17-year-old self-taught full stack systems developer and web programmer. He engineers low-level systems (operating system kernels, compilers, microVM virtualization runtimes) alongside high-performance web applications and distributed APIs.

## Core Projects
- **Track**: A deterministic systems programming language compiler targeting LLVM 22, featuring linear ownership, compile-time borrow checking, and zero-cost abstractions. Developed in Rust.
- **Carv**: A statically-typed systems programming language compiling to C99 specifically targeting ARM Cortex-M microcontrollers and host x86_64 architectures. Developed in Go.
- **Ignite**: A hardware-isolated microVM sandboxing runtime in Rust that boots Guest OS kernels using Firecracker KVM/Virtualization.framework to securely execute untrusted JS/TS code with direct VSOCK communication.
- **Karion OS**: A bare-metal x86 operating system kernel written in Rust featuring a custom physical memory allocator, memory paging, a virtual shell, block filesystem, BASIC interpreter, text editor, and games.
- **GBemu**: A cycle-accurate Game Boy emulator written in Zig with zero external dependencies, cycle-accurate CPU instruction emulation, scanline PPU rendering, and custom memory bank controller (MBC1) support.
- **Zario**: A minimal, fast structured JSON logging library for TypeScript/Bun with child loggers, multiple transports, and non-blocking asynchronous writes.
- **Kiattp**: A zero-dependency, lightweight HTTP client.

## Tech Stack
- Languages: TypeScript, JavaScript, Rust, Go, C, Assembly (x86/ARM), Python, Lua, Zig
- Web & Rts: React, Next.js, Astro, TailwindCSS, HTML5, CSS3, WebAssembly, Bun, Node.js
- Systems & Virtual: KVM, Firecracker, Virtualization.framework, Linux Kernel, Docker, Kubernetes, Git
- Databases: PostgreSQL, Redis, MongoDB, Custom Block Filesystems
